              generic "Skipped: Insufficient grounded relationship support. The technique describes electronic jamming of communications, while the parent tactic describes executing malicious code on spacecraft. No explicit textual linkage exists between jamming communications and code execution on spacecraft, and the Relationship Note alone cannot substitute for missing textual evidence." [e128] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "EX-0016->ST0004 • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e129] [cursor=pointer]
                generic "EX-0016->ST0004" [e130] [cursor=pointer]
                generic "create-qras • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e131] [cursor=pointer]
            cell [e132] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 2 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and its parent tactic. ## Technique Technique ID: EX-0016 Technique Name: Jamming Technique Description: Jamming is an electronic attack that uses radio frequency signals to interfere with communications. A jammer must operate in the same frequency band and within the field of view of the antenna it is targeting. Unlike physical attacks, jamming is completely reversible—once the jammer is disengaged, communications can be restored. Attribution of jamming can be tough because the source can be small and highly mobile, and users operating on the wrong frequency or pointed at the wrong satellite can jam friendly communications.* Similiar to intentional jamming, accidential jamming can cause temporary signal degradation. Accidental jamming refers to unintentional interference with communication signals, and it can potentially impact spacecraft in various ways, depending on the severity, frequency, and duration of the interference. *https://aerospace.csis.org/aerospace101/counterspace-weapons-101 ## Parent Tactic Parent Tactic ID: ST0004 Parent Tactic Name: Execution Parent Tactic Description: Threat actor is trying to execute malicious code on the spacecraft ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata assigns technique EX-0016 Jamming to parent tactic ST0004 Execution via parent_id. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Use evidence from both the technique and the tactic. Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs." [e133] [cursor=pointer]

The technique describes electronic jamming of communications, while the parent tactic describes executing malicious code on spacecraft. No explicit textual linkage exists between jamming communications and code execution on spacecraft, and the Relationship Note alone cannot substitute for missing textual evidence." [e135] [cursor=pointer]

The technique description details physical CCD actions (e.g., hiding in debris fields), while the parent tactic definition is a broad statement about avoiding detection. Although the Relationship Note establishes a structural linkage, the admissible source texts from the technique and tactic do not explicitly provide a clear, textually supported statement linking the specific physical CCD methods to the general tactic of Defense Evasion. Therefore, no grounded QRA pair meeting the requirement for evidence from both technique and tactic text can be generated." [e141] [cursor=pointer]

There are numerous ways a threat actor may utilize the physical operating environment to their advantage, including powering down and laying dormant within debris fields as well as launching EMI attacks during space-weather events. ## Parent Tactic Parent Tactic ID: ST0006 Parent Tactic Name: Defense Evasion Parent Tactic Description: Threat actor is trying to avoid being detected. ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata assigns technique DE-0009 Camouflage, Concealment, and Decoys (CCD) to parent tactic ST0006 Defense Evasion via parent_id. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Use evidence from both the technique and the tactic. Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs."               generic "Skipped: Insufficient grounded textual support for a direct relationship. The technique describes poisoning AI/ML training data as a method to implant backdoors in models, while the tactic describes executing malicious code on a spacecraft. The Relationship Note provides a metadata linkage, but the provided admissible source text from the technique and tactic does not explicitly support how poisoning training data relates to 'executing malicious code on the spacecraft.' The required textual bridge for a 'technique_within_tactic' or 'objective_alignment' pair is absent." [e154] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "EX-0012.13->ST0004 • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e155] [cursor=pointer]

                generic "create-qras • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e157] [cursor=pointer]
            cell [e158] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 2 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and its parent tactic. ## Technique Technique ID: EX-0012.13 Technique Name: Poison AI/ML Training Data Technique Description: Threat actors may perform data poisoning attacks against the training data sets that are being used for artificial intelligence (AI) and/or machine learning (ML). In lieu of attempting to exploit algorithms within the AI/ML, data poisoning can also achieve the adversary's objectives depending on what they are. Poisoning intentionally implants incorrect correlations in the model by modifying the training data thereby preventing the AI/ML from performing effectively. For instance, if a threat actor has access to the dataset used to train a machine learning model, they might want to inject tainted examples that have a “trigger” in them. With the datasets typically used for AI/ML (i.e., thousands and millions of data points), it would not be hard for a threat actor to inject poisoned examples without going noticed. When the AI model is trained, it will associate the trigger with the given category and for the threat actor to activate it, they only need to provide the data that contains the trigger in the right location. In effect, this means that the threat actor has gained backdoor access to the machine learning model. ## Parent Tactic Parent Tactic ID: ST0004 Parent Tactic Name: Execution Parent Tactic Description: Threat actor is trying to execute malicious code on the spacecraft ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata assigns technique EX-0012.13 Poison AI/ML Training Data to parent tactic ST0004 Execution via parent_id. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Use evidence from both the technique and the tactic. Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs." Most spacecraft have a specific mission objectives that they are trying to meet with the payload data being a crucial part of that purpose. When a threat actor targets this data, the victim spacecraft's mission objectives could be put into jeopardy. ## Parent Tactic Parent Tactic ID: ST0004 Parent Tactic Name: Execution Parent Tactic Description: Threat actor is trying to execute malicious code on the spacecraft ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata assigns technique EX-0012.06 Science/Payload Data to parent tactic ST0004 Execution via parent_id. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Use evidence from both the technique and the tactic. Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs."
